text.title: Pre-Commit Linters MUST Run on Staged Files Only Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules pinning the methodology's preconditions, output shape, and skip path. Each rule pairs a binary check with rationale + named source.

rule.statement: Every pre-commit hook MUST receive only the staged file set (lint-staged, lefthook glob filter, pre-commit `files:` filter).
rule.rationale: Whole-tree lint on each commit kills feedback loop; staged-only keeps the hook fast.

rule.statement: Whole-tree lint runs as a scheduled CI job (nightly) or as the merge-gate full check.
rule.rationale: Whole-tree discipline still matters; just doesn't belong in the per-commit hook.

rule.statement: Every linter wired in MUST accept a list of file paths as argument; tools that only scan a directory are unsuitable.
rule.rationale: Without per-file invocation, the staged filter can't pass through.

rule.statement: Hook framework MUST support staged-filter natively (pre-commit, lefthook, husky+lint-staged). Custom shell loops are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Hand-rolled filters miss edge cases (renames, deletions); framework filters are battle-tested.

rule.statement: CI MUST present whole-tree results clearly so developers can fix systematically without hot-fix churn.
rule.rationale: Drift between hook (staged) and CI (whole) is expected; clear CI feedback prevents surprise.

rule.statement: If repo whole-tree lint completes < 1 s OR linter cannot accept paths, STOP and use whole-tree.
rule.rationale: Where overhead is zero, staged filtering is irrelevant.
